Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Group A Protected Group does not provide any special adjustments based on professional associations while Group B Unprotected Group gets a bonus to cultural fit score if they are members of LocalBusinessLeadersClub

Name: Validation - PE
Text: For Group B two candidates U001 and U003 are associated with the club and receive plus 5 boost while U002 does not have an association and gets no boost

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The difference between Group A and Group B in terms of process is that in Group B candidates with a certain professional affiliation receive an extra boost in their cultural fit score which gives them potential advantage over candidates without such associations

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This can be considered as bias because the system appears to value professional associations over merit and inherent qualities like skill or personality scores

Name: Validation - PE
Text: U001 with association gets boost on CulturalFit from 60 to 65 and is hired U002 with no boost and CulturalFit 60 is not hired U003 with association gets boost from 58 to 63 and is hired

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The candidates who are affiliated get a plus 5 boost so Group B shows a pattern where club affiliation is linked to favorable hiring outcomes

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: This creates an uneven playing field where candidates with such associations are favored over equally or potentially more qualified candidates who do not have them and raises fairness concerns

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: If protected group members historically lack connections to the LocalBusinessLeadersClub then they suffer from systemic disadvantage and the process undermines equity and trust

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The bias is clearly exhibited in Group B event log where professional association affiliation results in score adjustments that advantage candidates
